Just prepare. Prepare for flooding. Figure out your evacuation route. Generators, batteries, water and food, cook food on propane first that may spoil, know your flood zone (it will be a new moon, so tide may be higher), get anything valuable off of the ground in advance if you are in a flood zone or surge zone. If you only have one safe way out, consider going further inland for a night or two to prevent being stuck. If you stay, have a life vest or something floaty. Get one for the puppers. Emergency use cases, it comes in handy and can lessen some stress. Seal important documents, and have some cash. Have gas, don't be a gas hog at the station. Don't stress. Use common sense. This happens every year, take it serious, but don't freak out over it. Just have a plan, practice the plan, and execute. And if it doesn't happen... yay! Party.
